content.full_text:
GABBAY ARCHITECTS
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION


YASSI E. GABBAY, D.P.L.G.-A.I.A.
HAMID E. GABBAY, A.I.A.

June 15, 1992


Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practice Council
428 "J" Street, Suite #500
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Re: Airplane ticket paid by a client.

Dear Mr. Hallabrin,

I am a Commissioner of the Planning Commission for the City of Beverly Hills. I am also a registered architect and a partner in the firm of Gabbay Architects. Our firm, beside California, has jobs in places such as Nevada, West Virginia and New York. Very often I have to visit the job sites and usually the airplane ticket is bought and paid for by the client.

Based on Form 721, on which Commissioners have to report all gifts and air tickets, I asked the F.P.P.C. and in particular Ms. Suzanne Barbarol if these tickets are considered gifts or should be considered income?

Ms. Barbarol, after consulting with legal divisions, informed that these tickets are considered income and NOT GIFTS. However, it was suggested that I get a written opinion. I am, therefore, cordially writing to you so that you may confirm the answer given to me.

With my best regards,

[signature]
Hamid E. Gabbay A.I.A


HEG:pt


448 SOUTH CANON DRIVE SUITE 200 • BEVERLY HILLS CA. 90212 TEL. [illegible] • FAX [illegible]
(310)553-8866    (310)201-0920

sections.question_synthetic: Are airplane tickets paid for by clients for job site visits considered gifts or income that must be reported on Form 721 by a Planning Commission member?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter from member seeking written confirmation of prior verbal guidance. No formal Q/A sections present. Document references prior FPPC phone consultation with Ms. Suzanne Barbaroli indicating tickets are income, not gifts. Requesting written opinion to confirm.
